title: Executive Order Imposing Economic Sanctions on Cuban Officials and Entities for Human Rights Violations and National Security Concerns
summary: This executive order authorizes the imposition of economic sanctions against individuals and entities within Cuba determined to be responsible for systematic repression of the Cuban population and for actions deemed to threaten United States national security and foreign policy interests. The order invokes authority granted to the President under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, the National Emergencies Act, and section 212(f) of the Immigration and Nationality Act of 1952, which provide mechanisms for restricting economic transactions, freezing assets, and implementing visa restrictions against designated targets. The sanctions regime would identify and target specific officials, military leaders, security force members, and state-owned enterprises involved in alleged human rights abuses and determined to pose security threats to the United States.

title: Presidential Nominations Submitted to Senate for Confirmation
summary: Announcement of multiple nominations sent to the United States Senate for confirmation, including David Brat of Virginia nominated as Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary to Australia, Hal Duncan of Texas nominated as Deputy Director of the Office of Management and Budget, and Sean Kaufman of Georgia nominated as Assistant Secretary for Preparedness and Response at the Department of Health and Human Services. These nominations proceed through the standard Senate confirmation process.

title: Presidential Nominations Sent to Senate for Various Federal Positions
summary: The President submitted nominations to the Senate for multiple federal appointments including Riley Barnes of Texas for Director of the Peace Corps, Rudolph Bauer of South Carolina for Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary to Belize, and Don Richard Berthiaume Jr. of Virginia for Inspector General of the Department of Justice, along with additional nominees for other federal positions. These nominations require Senate confirmation before the nominees can assume their respective offices.
